executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Status and Trends

National Medicaid enrollment totaled 75.7 million beneficiaries as of December 2025, with most states experiencing enrollment declines following the end of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. California leads with 12.6 million enrollees despite a notable 5.6% year-over-year decrease, while Illinois recorded the steepest decline at 6.5%. Texas maintained the third-largest enrollment at 4.1 million beneficiaries with a relatively modest 2.2% decrease, positioning it better than several peer states including Florida (-4.0%) and Ohio (-3.8%). Only North Carolina among major states showed growth at 1.6%, suggesting successful retention strategies worth examining. National spending reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a per-enrollee average of $2,851 and federal funding covering approximately 66% of total costs.

Per-enrollee spending patterns reveal significant state variation, with Pennsylvania ($4,368), Massachusetts ($4,185), and Ohio ($3,522) demonstrating the highest costs, while Florida ($2,029) and Illinois ($2,708) show the most efficient spending profiles. Texas spending of $2,950 per enrollee falls slightly above the national average and compares reasonably to peer states, though it exceeds more cost-effective programs like North Carolina ($2,613). Quality performance presents a concerning picture nationally, with an average score of only 65.6 out of 100 points in 2024. Texas scored 62 points, ranking 30th nationally and falling into the moderate performance tier, lagging behind top performers like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80) but avoiding the bottom tier occupied by southern states including Mississippi (48) and Alabama (52). Texas demonstrates strong managed care penetration at 93.3%, well above the national average of 83.2%, suggesting effective care coordination infrastructure that should theoretically support better quality outcomes than currently achieved.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.08 million enrollees, representing 5.4% of the national total. The state demonstrates strong operational efficiency through its managed care model, achieving 93.3% penetration compared to the 83.2% national average—one of the highest rates among large states. Per-enrollee spending of $2,950 remains close to the national average of $2,851 while delivering care to a massive population, indicating reasonable cost control. Texas's -2.2% year-over-year enrollment decline aligns with the national trend of unwinding pandemic-era continuous coverage, performing better than California (-5.6%) and Illinois (-6.5%) but trailing North Carolina's +1.6% growth. Among non-expansion peer states, Texas significantly outperforms Florida's much lower per-enrollee spending of $2,029, suggesting more comprehensive benefit coverage within existing eligibility constraints.

However, quality outcomes remain a persistent challenge for Texas, with a score of 62/100 ranking 30th nationally—well below the 65.6 national average and significantly trailing top performers like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80). This places Texas among southern peers like Georgia and North Carolina in the "moderate" performance tier, but ahead of regional neighbors like Arkansas (54) and Oklahoma (51). The state's quality performance represents a critical area requiring attention, particularly given the scale of the program and robust managed care infrastructure that should theoretically support better outcomes. Key factors to monitor include how Texas leverages its high managed care penetration to drive quality improvements, the impact of ongoing federal regulatory changes around interoperability and prior authorization, and whether the state pursues Medicaid expansion—which could both increase enrollment significantly and potentially improve overall health outcomes through expanded access to preventive care.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,368 significantly exceeds the national average of $2,851, indicating potential cost efficiency concerns.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi have quality scores below 55, with Mississippi at only 48 out of 100.

alerts.title: Florida Achieves Lowest Per-Enrollee Costs Nationally
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,029 is 29% below the national average, representing potential best practices in cost management.

alerts.title: High-Spending States Deliver Mixed Quality Outcomes
alerts.description: Pennsylvania spends $4,368 per enrollee but quality data is not available, while Massachusetts spends $4,185 and achieves top-tier quality scores.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid quality score ranks #30, below national average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scores 62/100 on quality measures, significantly below the national average of 65.6 and ranking 30th out of 50 states. This places Texas in the 'moderate' tier but closer to the low performer threshold of 60.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance creates regulatory risk and potential penalties for HHSC, while also presenting opportunities for Accenture to propose quality improvement initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement opportunities to HHSC leadership

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs $99 above national average at $2,950
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $2,950 per Medicaid enrollee compared to the national average of $2,851, representing a $99 premium per member. With 4.08M enrollees, this translates to approximately $404M in above-average spending.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher than average costs indicate potential inefficiencies that Accenture could help address through cost management strategies and operational improvements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost management opportunities with HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: Texas managed care penetration at 93.3% exceeds national 83.2%
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3.86M enrollees in managed care, significantly above the national average of 83.2%. This represents strong managed care adoption but may indicate areas for optimization.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration creates opportunities for Accenture to focus on managed care optimization, provider network management, and care coordination improvements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review managed care optimization strategies with HHSC

executiveInsights.title: CMS proposing new interoperability and prior authorization rules
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows CMS proposing new Medicare and Medicaid interoperability standards and prior authorization requirements. These regulatory changes will impact state Medicaid programs including Texas.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: New federal requirements will require HHSC to update systems and processes, creating implementation opportunities for Accenture's technology and compliance services.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag upcoming regulatory compliance needs to HHSC IT leadership

executiveInsights.title: Texas enrollment declined 2.2% YoY, better than most large states
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 2.2% year-over-year to 4.08M, outperforming larger declines in CA (-5.6%), IL (-6.5%), and FL (-4.0%). Only NC showed growth at +1.6% among major states.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Relatively stable enrollment suggests effective redetermination processes but ongoing decline may impact HHSC revenue and require program adjustments.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review enrollment retention strategies with HHSC eligibility team

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration creates major contract renewal opportunities but high displacement risk.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish early intelligence on MCO contract timelines and develop differentiated value propositions.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality measure gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, indicating significant improvement needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop targeted quality improvement solutions addressing Texas's specific performance gaps.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 2.2% year-over-year, creating cost pressure and operational challenges.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position cost optimization and efficiency solutions to help HHSC manage declining revenues.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG oversight escalation
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG oversight and work plan items create compliance risks but also modernization opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Proactively align current engagements with OIG priorities and develop compliance-first positioning.

riskOpportunity.label: Interoperability mandate implementation
riskOpportunity.description: New federal interoperability and prior authorization standards require significant system upgrades.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Lead with proven interoperability solutions and offer comprehensive implementation services.

riskOpportunity.label: HHSC procurement pipeline
riskOpportunity.description: Active Texas HHSC procurement portal indicates continuous opportunities but high competition.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish systematic monitoring of HHSC and ESBD portals with rapid response capabilities.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-enrollee cost optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's $2,950 per-enrollee cost is above national average, creating efficiency improvement pressure.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop analytics-driven cost reduction strategies targeting high-impact service areas.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive displacement risk
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple competitor tracking of Texas opportunities increases risk of losing current positions.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen existing client relationships through expanded value delivery and strategic advisory roles.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 4,083,939 Medicaid enrollees as of December 2025.

pulseMetrics.label: Enrollment Change
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas enrollment declined 2.2% year-over-year, outperforming most large states.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $2,950 per Medicaid enrollee annually in fiscal year 2024.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 enrollees in managed care.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on quality measures, ranking 30th nationally in moderate tier.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two active Texas procurement portals are being monitored for Medicaid opportunities.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Six federal regulatory and oversight items are currently tracked affecting Medicaid programs.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific milestone dates are provided in the current data.
